10.anchor: But the day of the Lord will come as unexpectedly as a thief. Then the heavens will pass away with a terrible noise, and the very elements themselves will disappear in fire, and the earth and everything on it will be found to deserve judgment.
10.note: Other manuscripts read will be burned up; will be found destroyed.

1.anchor: I am writing to you who share the same precious faith we have. This faith was given to you because of the justice and fairness
1.note: Or to you in the righteousness.

13.anchor: And it is only right that I should keep on reminding you as long as I live.
13.note: Greek as long as I am in this tent tabernacle

14.anchor: For our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me that I must soon leave this earthly life,
14.note: Greek I must soon put off my tent tabernacle

17.anchor: when he received honor and glory from God the Father. The voice from the majestic glory of God said to him, “This is my dearly loved Son, who brings me great joy.”

19.anchor: Because of that experience, we have even greater confidence in the message proclaimed by the prophets. You must pay close attention to what they wrote, for their words are like a lamp shining in a dark place—until the Day dawns, and Christ the Morning Star shines

20.anchor: Above all, you must realize that no prophecy in Scripture ever came from the prophet’s own understanding,
20.note: Or is a matter of one’s own interpretation.

4.anchor: For God did not spare even the angels who sinned. He threw them into hell,

4.note: Some manuscripts read in chains of gloom.

10.anchor: These people are proud and arrogant, daring even to scoff at supernatural beings

11.anchor: But the angels, who are far greater in power and strength, do not dare to bring from the Lord
11.note: Other manuscripts read to the Lord;

13.anchor: Their destruction is their reward for the harm they have done. They love to indulge in evil pleasures in broad daylight. They are a disgrace and a stain among you. They delight in deception
13.note: Some manuscripts read in fellowship meals.

15.anchor: They have wandered off the right road and followed the footsteps of Balaam son of Beor,

22.anchor: They prove the truth of this proverb: “A dog returns to its vomit.”
